questions.question: What is the primary difference between an ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) and a Buyer Persona?

options.text: ICP is for marketing teams, Persona is for sales teams.
ICP describes the Company (Firmographics); Persona describes the Human (Role/Psychographics).
ICP is based on real data; Persona is entirely fictional.
There is no difference; they are synonyms.

questions.explanation: ICP defines which accounts to target (firmographics). Persona defines who to talk to within those accounts (psychographics).

questions.question: Which member of the Buying Committee typically holds the veto power on technical or compliance grounds, but cannot authorize the purchase?

options.text: The Economic Buyer
The User Buyer
The Technical Evaluator
The Blocker

questions.explanation: The Technical Evaluator (Gatekeeper) ensures the solution fits the tech stack and compliance rules. They can kill a deal but rarely sign the check.

questions.question: Who is usually the best entry point (Champion) for a 'Wedge' strategy?

options.text: The CEO (Economic Buyer)
The User Buyer
The HR Manager
The Board of Directors

questions.explanation: The User Buyer feels the daily pain most acutely. Winning them over allows you to build a bottom-up case to present to the Economic Buyer.

questions.question: What is the 'Solo Founder Advantage' when dealing with Technical Evaluators?

options.text: You can offer them equity in your company.
You can speak peer-to-peer about architecture and security, building trust.
You can afford to take them to expensive dinners.
You have a larger team than they do.

questions.explanation: Technical Evaluators respect technical expertise. A founder who knows the stack can bypass the skepticism usually directed at non-technical sales reps.

questions.question: Why is it important to identify the 'Blocker'?

options.text: So you can get them fired.
So you can ignore them.
So you can understand their threat perception and neutralize it or win them over.
So you can send them spam emails.

questions.explanation: Blockers retard the sale because they feel threatened. Understanding their motivation allows you to position your tool as an asset to them, not a threat.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of defining a 'Follower Persona' for a creator business?

options.text: To increase follower counts for vanity metrics.
To identify exactly what content to create that leads an audience member toward a purchase.
To block people from seeing your content if they don't have money.
To replace the ICP entirely.

questions.explanation: Followers consume content but may not be ready to buy. Defining their persona helps you build the 'Transformation Bridge' needed to convert interest into a transaction.

questions.question: Describe ONE specific buyer persona within your ICP. Include their job title, daily challenges, what success looks like for them, and their typical objections. If you are a creator, also describe your primary 'Follower' and what bridge is needed to move them to a customer.
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the buyer/follower persona depth. Check for: (1) specific role definition, (2) real daily challenges/gaps, (3) clear success metrics/transformations, (4) realistic objections. Suggest improvements.
